Yongbyon Nuclear Complex.name: Yongbyon Nuclear Complex
Yongbyon Nuclear Complex.note: Sustained modernization and expansion throughout 2025, including a suspected new uranium enrichment facility, 5 MWe reactor makeover and resumed operations, and construction of a second underground radioactive waste storage facility. IAEA confirmed expansion in March 2026.

Sinpo South Shipyard.name: Sinpo South Shipyard
Sinpo South Shipyard.note: Sinpo-C class submarine (Hero Kim Kun Ok) left drydock in Feb 2025 but remains non-operational after 2+ years. New 8m-diameter submarine hull components observed in materials staging area indicating a second SSB under construction.

THAAD Site — Seongju County.name: THAAD Site — Seongju County
THAAD Site — Seongju County.note: US redeployed THAAD interceptor missiles and possibly launcher vehicles from Seongju to the Middle East in early March 2026. Only one of six launchers confirmed returned to base as of March 13. South Korea's high-altitude missile defense coverage temporarily degraded.

Sohae Satellite Launch Station.name: Sohae Satellite Launch Station
Sohae Satellite Launch Station.note: Engine test of a smaller upper-stage rocket engine observed in late September 2025. Massive infrastructure expansion continues: new horizontal processing building externally complete, new seaport docking slip finished, railroad spur line under construction.

Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site.name: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site.note: Site remains well-maintained and test-ready at Tunnel No. 3. Engineering building outside Tunnel No. 3 was razed mid-2024. Flood damage from summer 2024 washed out access bridges. Most preparations for a test have been completed; timing depends on political decision.

Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border).name: Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border)
Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border).note: Passenger rail service resumed December 2024 after five-year hiatus. New 1.3 km road bridge construction shows significant progress, expected operational Q1 2026. Railcar traffic shifted toward more ore/tank cars. Approx 225 railcars observed at Khasan facility.

Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border).name: Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border)
Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border).note: China-DPRK exchanges accelerated since Kim-Xi summit in September 2025. Beijing-Pyongyang passenger train service resumed March 12, 2026. China broke ground on new Dandong cross-border trade processing park in January 2026. New Yalu River Bridge port-of-entry advancing.

Rason SEZ.name: Rason SEZ
Rason SEZ.note: Rason reopened to international tourists in January 2025, the first non-Russian visitors since pre-pandemic. Select Chinese merchants granted access by November 2025. Cautious revival of economic activity but zone has not yet returned to pre-pandemic investment levels.
